title: Record doc rendering workflow notes
description: Document reproducible commands/notes for rendering docs/architecture-guide and troubleshooting diagram layout so future sessions can continue without setup drift.
close_reason: Completed: documented reproducible render command and Mermaid PDF troubleshooting notes in diagrams-and-flows.qmd

title: Rewrite architecture guide for junior engineers
description: Expand docs/architecture-guide into a coffee-table read for junior engineers: explain the architecture benefits, why WASM is attractive, and why Conduit uses Asupersync instead of a broader runtime surface.
close_reason: Expanded the architecture guide for junior-engineer reading, including architecture benefits, WASM rationale, and the Asupersync vs Tokio boundary; re-rendered the guide.

title: Epic 14: Release Candidate Automation and Packaging
description: Turn the documented release checklist into repeatable project automation and a source-release handoff path. This epic should make validation reproducible, package intent explicit, and release notes auditable without changing the current publish=false policy.
close_reason: Completed release candidate automation and packaging epic: CI validation gate is checked in and documented, CLI help/completion surface is stable, package metadata and artifact intent are audited, source-checkpoint release notes template is available, and closed-bead working tree hygiene was handled.

title: ci-full-validation-gate
description: Add a checked-in CI or equivalent automation path for the documented full validation gate. Acceptance: format, workspace check, workspace tests, strict clippy, Dylint, bench compile checks, and git diff whitespace are represented; docs/validation-matrix.md links to the automation; local Nix/devshell assumptions are explicit.
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/ci.yml to run the documented validation gate through the Nix devshell and updated docs/validation-matrix.md to link the workflow and explain the local Dylint behavior.

title: release-notes-source-checkpoint
description: Create a source-checkpoint release notes template for the current 0.1.0-style repository handoff. Acceptance: notes summarize capabilities, validation run, known deferred work, and artifact policy; docs/release-readiness.md points to the notes template or generated example.
close_reason: Added docs/release-notes-source-checkpoint-template.md for 0.1.0-style source checkpoint handoffs, covering capabilities, validation run results, artifact policy, known deferred work, Beads snapshot, and handoff notes. Linked it from docs/release-readiness.md and README. Verified cargo fmt --all --check and git diff --check.

title: workspace-package-metadata-audit
description: Audit workspace package metadata against release-readiness expectations. Acceptance: every crate inherits version/license/publish policy, LICENSE matches Cargo.toml, binary/source artifact intent is documented, and any package metadata gaps are fixed or explicitly recorded.
close_reason: Audited workspace package metadata: all root workspace crates inherit version, edition, license, and publish policy; LICENSE matches MIT; documented source-only artifact intent and deferred public-registry metadata gaps in docs/package-metadata-audit-2026-05-09.md. Verified cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1, cargo fmt --all --check, and git diff --check.

title: cli-help-and-completion-surface
description: Decide and implement the stable CLI help/completion surface for release handoff. Acceptance: help output is intentional and test-covered; shell completion generation is either implemented or explicitly deferred; README/docs mention the supported path.
close_reason: Implemented Commands::Completions{shell} via clap_complete::generate; added bash/zsh/fish/powershell/elvish support; binary renamed to conduit via [[bin]]; help and completions tests added; README updated; all 214 tests pass, clippy clean

title: commit-closed-bead-working-tree
description: Commit the in-progress working tree changes from closed beads (cdt-pl3.5 and cdt-pmb.*) before opening the next bead. Eight source files and seven doc files are uncommitted. Acceptance: jj diff (or git status) shows no uncommitted changes from closed beads; working copy is clean before a new bead is opened. Source: Audit_2026_05_07 AB-18.
close_reason: Frozen the closed-bead working tree by preparing the current JJ change as the completed cdt-pl3.5/cdt-pl3.6/cdt-pmb/audit-backlog checkpoint before opening new implementation work.

title: Epic 16: Node Authoring SDK and Templates
description: Reduce the friction of writing real Conduit nodes. Provide reusable native-node helpers, a documented WASM guest template, contract/capability authoring guidance, and a repeatable template-to-run path.
close_reason: Completed node authoring SDK/template epic: reusable native helpers, WASM guest template docs, contract/capability authoring guide, template-to-CLI WASM smoke path, contract property coverage, and node authoring error-pattern guidance are all in place.

title: native-node-helper-apis
description: Add reusable native-node helper APIs or test-kit builders for common source, transform, sink, fan-out, and fan-in nodes. Acceptance: examples stop duplicating low-level port draining/sending boilerplate; helper behavior is covered by focused tests; core executor semantics remain unchanged.
close_reason: Added drain_port async helper, test_packet builder, SinkExecutor (records all received packets), and SourceExecutor (sends configured byte payloads) to conduit-test-kit; conduit-runtime added as dev-dep for async tests; 4 new focused tests with real channels; 233 tests pass, clippy clean

title: wasm-guest-template-and-wit-docs
description: Turn the uppercase guest fixture into a documented authoring template. Acceptance: WIT contract, expected batch input/output JSON shape, build command, target requirements, and fixture layout are documented; template guidance avoids committing generated target output.
close_reason: Created uppercase-guest/README.md covering WIT contract, batch I/O JSON shape with test vector examples, build command, target requirements table, fixture layout, step-by-step authoring guide, and gitignore guidance; added .gitignore excluding target/ and *.wasm; updated fixtures/README.md and root README with link to authoring guide

title: contract-capability-authoring-guide
description: Document how authors should map a workflow node to contracts, port schemas, execution modes, determinism, retry metadata, and capability descriptors. Acceptance: guide includes native advisory versus WASM strict enforcement language and at least one complete example matching the runnable workflows.
close_reason: Added docs/contract-capability-authoring.md with native/WASM enforcement guidance, field-by-field authoring guidance, complete wasm-uppercase contract/capability example, advisory native effects example, common validation failures, and links from README/workflow guide; verified git diff --check.

title: template-to-cli-wasm-smoke-path
description: Provide a repeatable template-to-run smoke path for a WASM node through the real CLI manifest surface. Acceptance: docs or scripts show build fixture, write manifest, run workflow, inspect metadata, and clean generated artifacts; validation uses the real component-loading path.
close_reason: Created examples/wasm-uppercase.workflow.json, examples/wasm-uppercase.components.json, and examples/wasm-uppercase.md covering all 7 smoke-path steps (build, validate-manifest, validate, explain, run, inspect metadata, clean); workflow and explain output verified against the CLI; docs/workflow-run-guide.md and root README updated with links

title: node-authoring-error-patterns
description: Capture recommended error patterns for native and WASM node authors. Acceptance: examples show user/internal visibility, retry disposition, cancellation handling, malformed payload rejection, and how those errors appear in metadata JSONL and run --json summaries.
close_reason: Added docs/node-authoring-error-patterns.md covering native and WASM error patterns, user/internal visibility, retry disposition, cancellation propagation, malformed payload rejection, and how errors appear in metadata JSONL and run --json; linked from README, workflow guide, and contract/capability guide; verified git diff --check.

title: quickcheck-schema-ref-and-contract-validation-invariants
description: Add quickcheck property tests for SchemaRef and contract validation invariants in conduit-contract. SchemaRef::new is a simple validator (reject empty/whitespace-only) with no generated coverage; contract port/capability alignment has no property coverage. Acceptance: a quickcheck property that any non-empty non-whitespace String round-trips through SchemaRef; a property that a NodeContract built from a matching WorkflowDefinition always passes validate_workflow_contracts; cargo test -p conduit-contract passes cleanly.
close_reason: Added QuickCheck property coverage for SchemaRef round-trips and matching workflow contract/capability validation invariants; verified cargo test -p conduit-contract.

title: Polish runtime and package sections
description: Apply the same smaller-table readability format to the runtime-model and package-contracts chapters so the remaining list-heavy sections read more like a professional guide.
close_reason: Extended the table-based readability pass to the runtime and package chapters and re-rendered the guide.

title: Epic 4: Contracts, Workflow Formats, and Inspection
description: Add node contracts, versioned external workflow definitions, parser isolation, and AI-inspectable workflow/contract projections. Source: docs/archetecture/proposal_final.md.

title: contracts-core
description: Add conduit-contract with node contracts, port contracts, schema refs, execution modes, declared capabilities, determinism, retry metadata, and contract validation.
close_reason: Implemented the contracts core crate, added workflow/contract validation, and verified the workspace with fmt, check, test, clippy, and dylint.

title: workflow-format-crate
description: Add conduit-workflow-format with versioned raw workflow definitions that parse into validated domain types without adding parser dependencies to core graph crates.
close_reason: Implemented parser-free conduit-workflow-format crate with versioned raw workflow definitions, typed format errors, conversion into validated workflow domain types, and tests.

title: workflow-format-json
description: Add canonical JSON workflow parsing, round-trip tests, and typed diagnostics for missing or unknown conduit_version.
close_reason: Implemented canonical JSON parsing and serialization for conduit-workflow-format with serde-backed raw structs, typed JSON/format errors, version diagnostics, unknown-field rejection, and round-trip tests.

title: workflow-format-toml
description: Add TOML workflow parsing behind a feature for human-authored local workflow files.

title: workflow-format-yaml
description: Add optional YAML workflow parsing behind a feature, isolated so parser maintenance risk does not affect core crates.

title: introspection-core
description: Add pure workflow/contract introspection data types covering nodes, ports, edge capacities, schemas, capabilities, enforcement level, determinism, and execution mode.

title: introspection-json-snapshots
description: Add JSON rendering for introspection behind serde and snapshot tests for stable AI-facing diagnostics.

title: cli-validate-inspect
description: Add validate and inspect CLI commands once contracts, workflow formats, and introspection data are available.

title: Fix Mermaid PDF pagination in architecture guide
description: Ensure Mermaid diagrams in docs/architecture-guide/system-overview.qmd fit on a single PDF page in _output/Conduit-Architecture-Guide.pdf by adjusting figure sizing/layout settings and verifying render output.
close_reason: Completed: adjusted Mermaid sizing in system-overview.qmd and re-rendered PDF successfully

title: Epic 7: High-Performance Data Tier
description: Defer Arrow/DataFusion until the byte-message runtime and WASM boundary are stable, then add high-performance payload support behind features with benchmarks. Source: docs/archetecture/proposal_final.md.

title: payload-arrow-feature
description: Add Arrow payload support behind an arrow feature after the byte-message runtime and WASM boundary are stable.
close_reason: Added optional Apache Arrow RecordBatch payload tier behind conduit-core arrow feature, with feature tests and WIT boundary rejection for unsupported payloads.

title: bench-backpressure-capacity
description: Add Criterion benchmarks for capacity 1, default capacity, and fan-out/fan-in throughput and latency behavior.

title: api-substrate-leak-check
description: Add a check that public Conduit APIs do not expose asupersync types, preserving the runtime substrate boundary.
close_reason: Added public API substrate leak regression test

title: Epic 18: Runtime Hardening and Regression Guardrails
description: Harden the completed FBP/WASM runtime surface with snapshot tests, failure matrices, public API boundary checks, watchdog policy coverage, and benchmark baselines so future feature work does not quietly regress execution semantics or metadata contracts.
close_reason: Completed runtime hardening guardrails: metadata snapshots and property tests, workflow graph/property coverage, introspection and error-code invariants, supervisor failure matrix, public API substrate boundary checks, watchdog policy docs, and initial Criterion baselines.

title: metadata-json-snapshot-tests
description: Add snapshot-style coverage for stable metadata JSONL and conduit run --json outputs. Acceptance: native run metadata and summary JSON have deterministic fixtures; schema-affecting changes fail tests until docs/metadata-json.md is updated intentionally.
close_reason: Added inline insta snapshots for deterministic CLI metadata JSONL and run --json summary output for the native two-node workflow; cargo test -p conduit-cli passes.

title: quickcheck-error-code-string-distinctness
description: Add a quickcheck property test that encodes ErrorCode string stability in conduit-core. The ErrorCode::as_str codes are the stable API strings committed to in docs (CDT-VAL-*, CDT-EXEC-*, etc.) but no test enforces their uniqueness or prefix conventions. Acceptance: a test exhausting all ErrorCode variants asserting every as_str() result is unique, non-empty, and matches its documented category prefix; cargo test -p conduit-core passes cleanly.
close_reason: Added an exhaustive ErrorCode string stability test covering uniqueness, non-empty values, and category prefixes; verified focused error-code test and cargo test -p conduit-core.

title: supervisor-failure-matrix-tests
description: Broaden workflow supervisor tests across failure and cancellation cases. Acceptance: tests cover failing source, failing transform, disconnected downstream, sibling cancellation, fan-out partial failure rejection, and summary/error metadata expectations.
close_reason: Broadened conduit-engine supervisor failure matrix tests for failing source, failing transform, disconnected downstream send failure, fan-out partial-send rejection, sibling cancellation, and summary/error metadata expectations; verified cargo test -p conduit-engine.

title: public-api-substrate-boundary-audit
description: Expand public API checks that keep asupersync and Wasmtime details behind Conduit-owned boundaries. Acceptance: tests or docs verify public node/port/runtime-facing APIs do not expose substrate types except in runtime/wasm adapter crates where intentional.
close_reason: Expanded public API substrate boundary checks to catch Wasmtime/asupersync substrate type leaks and enforce adapter wrapper names are only public in conduit-runtime/conduit-wasm; verified cargo test -p conduit-core.

title: watchdog-policy-cli-and-docs
description: Decide how no-progress watchdog policy should be configured outside tests. Acceptance: either expose a CLI run option with documented behavior and tests, or document why watchdog policy remains library-only for now; release docs reflect the decision.
close_reason: Documented that no-progress watchdog policy remains library-only for this release, including CLI rationale and release-readiness note; verified git diff --check.

title: criterion-baseline-capture
description: Capture an initial benchmark baseline for metadata overhead and backpressure capacity. Acceptance: run measurement benches, summarize Criterion output in a lightweight docs note, and update benchmark operations guidance with baseline comparison expectations.
close_reason: Ran Criterion measurement benches for conduit-core metadata overhead and conduit-engine backpressure capacity, captured the 2026-05-08 baseline note, and updated benchmark comparison guidance; verified git diff --check.

title: introspection-unit-coverage
description: Expand conduit-introspection test coverage. The crate is 938 lines and powers conduit inspect and conduit explain, but has only 4 unit tests. Acceptance: at least one test for each error path in introspect_workflow (unknown node contract, port direction mismatch); a snapshot test (using insta) for workflow_introspection_to_json_string output for a workflow containing both native and WASM nodes; cargo test -p conduit-introspection passes cleanly. Source: Audit_2026_05_07 AB-15.
close_reason: Expanded conduit-introspection coverage with missing-contract and port-direction mismatch error tests plus a mixed native/WASM JSON snapshot under the serde feature; verified cargo test -p conduit-introspection and cargo test -p conduit-introspection --features serde.

title: public-api-substrate-test-hardening
description: Harden the public API substrate boundary test in conduit-core/tests/public_api_substrate.rs. The current source-text pattern scan can false-positive on unrelated type names (e.g. any pub type named Runtime or SendPermit). Acceptance: add an explicit allowlist of acceptable matches OR adopt a symbol-level check (e.g. cargo-public-api); the test still catches a deliberate pub struct Foo { inner: asupersync::Runtime }; cargo test -p conduit-core passes cleanly. Source: Audit_2026_05_07 AB-16.
close_reason: Hardened public API substrate leak detection by exact-token matching bare substrate identifiers, preserving qualified asupersync path checks, and adding regression tests for qualified leaks, bare imported leaks, and unrelated identifier substrings; verified cargo test -p conduit-core and cargo test -p conduit-core --test public_api_substrate.

title: quickcheck-workflow-adversarial-graph-generation
description: Add quickcheck property tests for conduit-workflow graph validation using adversarial generated topologies. The existing proptest covers only valid linear chains; cycle detection, duplicate-port rejection, fan-out, fan-in, and disconnected-node handling have no generated coverage. Acceptance: a quickcheck Arbitrary impl or generator strategy that builds random directed graphs from pools of valid node/port IDs; assertions that the validator never panics and returns consistent error variants for each invalid condition; valid fan-out and fan-in topologies accepted; cargo test -p conduit-workflow passes cleanly.
close_reason: Added QuickCheck workflow validation coverage for generated acyclic/disconnected graphs, fan-in/fan-out acceptance, and invalid-condition error variant consistency without panics; verified cargo test -p conduit-workflow.

title: quickcheck-metadata-record-serialization-contract
description: Add quickcheck property tests for metadata_record_to_json_value output contract in conduit-core. The JSONL output from conduit run is a stable machine-facing contract with no generated coverage. Acceptance: a quickcheck Arbitrary impl for MetadataRecord variants; assertions that serialization never panics, the 'kind' key is always present with a known value, and reproducibility-sensitive fields (timestamp, thread_id, process id, raw payload bytes) are absent from every record; cargo test -p conduit-core passes cleanly.
close_reason: Added QuickCheck coverage for metadata JSON serialization across all MetadataRecord variants, including top-level kind validation and recursive reproducibility-sensitive key exclusion; verified cargo test -p conduit-core.

title: Trim README reference lists
description: Restructure the top-level README into a cleaner start-here layout and trim the long reference lists at the bottom.
close_reason: Trimmed the README reference lists into a cleaner start-here layout with shorter docs and examples sections.

title: Continue architecture guide polish pass
description: Refine the remaining architecture-guide sections into a more polished, readable format while preserving the content and rendering output.
close_reason: Extended the remaining guide sections to the same themed table format and re-rendered the architecture guide.

title: Epic 1: Conduit Foundation
description: Establish the smallest honest base for Conduit so later work has stable types, validation rules, runtime boundaries, and documentation for foundational decisions. Source draft: docs/epics/epic-1-foundation.md
close_reason: All planned foundation beads cdt-dmh.1 through cdt-dmh.8 are complete and verified; follow-on work has been split into post-foundation cleanup and runtime-bootstrap planning.

title: Workspace skeleton
description: Create the Rust workspace layout, crate boundaries, shared developer entry points, and minimal repo plumbing. Acceptance: workspace builds, crates resolve cleanly, no behavior beyond smoke-level code. Verso target: short architecture note for crate boundaries.
close_reason: Completed before Beads initialization during initial Conduit bootstrap; verified with workspace tests, strict Clippy, and Dylint.

title: Identity primitives
description: Define validated WorkflowId, NodeId, and PortId primitives plus associated parsing, formatting, and generation rules. Acceptance: types are distinct, minimal, invariant-protecting, and tested. Verso target: why each ID exists and what bug it prevents.
close_reason: Completed before Beads initialization as the first foundation type bead; validated opaque IDs with constructors, display/parse support, unit tests, strict Clippy, and Dylint.

title: Workflow model
description: Define workflow definition types, node and edge model, port topology, and graph-level structure. Acceptance: valid workflows are representable, invalid structural graphs are rejected, and model remains separate from runtime behavior. Verso target: graph shape and structural versus semantic constraints.
close_reason: Implemented validated workflow model with node, port, edge, and graph structure.

title: Execution context and message envelope
description: Define runtime-facing context, message/payload envelope, execution metadata, and cancellation/lifecycle hooks at the type boundary. Acceptance: runtime communication boundary is concrete but does not hard-code engine implementation too early. Verso target: why envelope/context fields exist.
close_reason: Implemented runtime context, message envelope, execution metadata, cancellation state, and lifecycle hook boundary types under degraded coordination.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable in this session; intended files: crates/conduit-core/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-engine/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-runtime/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-workflow/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-cli/src/main.rs

title: Capability and boundary types
description: Define types that express what a node may receive, emit, or do, including capability descriptors and permission-like boundaries. Acceptance: capability rules are explicit, separate from workflow structure, and invalid combinations are rejected. Verso target: runtime safety and isolation tradeoffs.
close_reason: Implemented explicit node capability descriptors for port and runtime effect boundaries with validation for duplicate and conflicting claims.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: degraded-coordination: Agent Mail unavailable in this session; intended files: crates/conduit-core/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/capability.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/context.rs, crates/conduit-core/src/message.rs, crates/conduit-engine/src/lib.rs, crates/conduit-runtime/src/lib.rs

title: Error model
description: Define shared error enums, error codes, user-facing versus internal classification, and recovery or retry categories if needed. Acceptance: errors are structured, stable enough for downstream use, and deterministically mapped. Verso target: error taxonomy and tradeoffs.
close_reason: Replaced the shared stringly-typed runtime result with a structured ConduitError taxonomy carrying stable codes, visibility, retry guidance, and typed conversions for validation failures.

title: Test kit
description: Add reusable builders, fixtures, fake nodes or test doubles, property-test helpers, and reusable invariants. Acceptance: later beads can test model and runtime behavior without repetition and helpers reduce test friction. Verso target: only non-obvious testing strategy.
close_reason: Added a dedicated conduit-test-kit crate with reusable builders and executor doubles, plus property tests for identifier and workflow invariants and improved engine error-path coverage.

title: Documentation pass
description: Add Verso annotations to decision-heavy foundation modules, ensure narrative matches code, and capture rationale while decisions are fresh. Acceptance: the why is close to code for all foundational decisions and docs do not drift from implementation.
close_reason: Added decision-focused module docs for workflow structure, runtime context, lifecycle hooks, capability boundaries, and error taxonomy, and verified the workspace gates remained green.

title: Epic 15: CLI and Workflow Authoring UX
description: Make Conduit easier to drive from the command line and from generated workflow artifacts: structured argument parsing, multi-format workflow loading, schema surfaces, component manifest validation, and richer runnable examples.
close_reason: Completed CLI and workflow authoring UX epic: clap command surface, TOML/YAML workflow loading, format round-trip coverage, manifest validation, JSON Schema generation for workflows and WASM manifests, and authoring examples pack are all in place and documented.

title: cli-migrate-to-clap
description: Replace the manual CLI argument parser with clap while preserving current command behavior and exit codes. Acceptance: validate, inspect, explain, run, --json, and --wasm-components have typed clap definitions; usage errors remain stable; unit tests cover parse behavior.
close_reason: Replaced hand-rolled match-based parser with clap derive macros. Added Cli/Commands structs with typed subcommands (validate, inspect, explain, run). dispatch_command() replaces command_output() with PathBuf args instead of raw &str. CliError::Usage removed; clap handles usage errors with exit code 2. Two tests updated to use Cli::try_parse_from + dispatch_command. All 12 CLI tests pass; full validation gate clean.

title: cli-load-toml-and-yaml-workflows
description: Let CLI commands load workflow documents through conduit-workflow-format instead of JSON-only helpers. Acceptance: JSON remains canonical; TOML is supported where the crate feature exists; YAML behavior is feature-gated or explicitly unavailable; validate/inspect/explain/run share the same loader and diagnostics.
close_reason: Added load_workflow(input,path) dispatcher branching on .json/.toml/.yaml/.yml extensions; enabled conduit-workflow-format toml+yaml features in conduit-cli; all four commands (validate/inspect/explain/run) share the loader; unsupported extensions give a clear error; 5 new tests; 227 tests pass, clippy clean

title: workflow-and-manifest-schema-generation
description: Expose machine-readable schemas for workflow documents and WASM component manifests. Acceptance: schema generation is isolated behind appropriate dependencies or tooling; generated schemas are documented for AI/tool consumers; unknown-field and version expectations are represented.
close_reason: Added CLI schema generation via conduit schema workflow and conduit schema wasm-manifest; schemas represent deny-unknown-field boundaries with additionalProperties=false, workflow conduit_version const 1, identifier shape, capacity minimum, and manifest component/fuel expectations. Documented AI/tool usage in docs/schema-generation.md and linked it from README and workflow-run-guide.md. Verified cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check -p conduit-cli --all-targets, cargo test -p conduit-cli, strict cargo clippy for conduit-cli, and git diff --check.

title: component-manifest-validate-command
description: Add an explicit CLI validation path for WASM component manifests before execution. Acceptance: manifest JSON rejects unknown fields, invalid node ids, duplicate node entries, missing workflow nodes, and unreadable component paths with stable user-facing errors; docs include the command.
close_reason: Added Commands::ValidateManifest{workflow,manifest} subcommand; validates unknown fields, invalid node IDs, duplicate entries, unreadable component paths, and missing workflow nodes (with --workflow); 7 new tests; README updated; 222 tests pass, clippy clean

title: workflow-authoring-examples-pack
description: Add a small authoring-oriented examples pack for common workflow shapes. Acceptance: examples include workflow files, expected inspect/explain output notes, and command snippets for native and WASM runs; docs/examples-catalog.md links them.
close_reason: Added examples/authoring pack with native fanout JSON workflow, native join YAML workflow, WASM uppercase workflow plus manifest, and README command snippets for validate, inspect, explain, native runs, and WASM runs. Linked the pack from docs/examples-catalog.md and README. Verified the new workflows with conduit validate/run, validate-manifest with workflow, inspect/explain spot checks, cargo fmt --all --check, and git diff --check.

title: workflow-format-yaml-toml-tests
description: Add YAML and TOML workflow parse tests to conduit-workflow-format before cdt-dtx.2 wires them into the CLI. serde_yml and toml are already workspace dependencies but no tests exercise those parse paths. Acceptance: one round-trip test per format — parse a minimal valid workflow from YAML, then TOML; assert structural equality with the JSON equivalent; cargo test -p conduit-workflow-format passes cleanly. Source: Audit_2026_05_07 AB-17.
close_reason: Added TOML and YAML tests that parse the same workflow shape as canonical JSON and assert validated WorkflowDefinition equality; verified default and toml,yaml feature test runs for conduit-workflow-format.

title: quickcheck-workflow-format-json-round-trip
description: Add quickcheck property tests for conduit-workflow-format JSON round-trip stability. The format crate has 16 unit tests and zero property coverage, but the JSON format is the external contract for every workflow file. Acceptance: a quickcheck Arbitrary impl for WorkflowDefinition; serialize to JSON, parse back, assert structural equality; property holds across varied node counts, edge topologies, and edge capacity variants; cargo test -p conduit-workflow-format passes cleanly.
close_reason: Added QuickCheck-backed generated workflow JSON round-trip coverage using validated acyclic WorkflowDefinition values with varied edge topologies and capacity policies; verified cargo test -p conduit-workflow-format.

title: external-effect-capability-and-metadata-record
description: Add the first-class external-effect vocabulary surfaced by the AI-call orchestration workload without introducing real network execution. Acceptance: core capability taxonomy can declare a generic external effect, metadata has a stable JSONL record shape for external-effect observations, docs explain advisory native semantics and no Arrow/DataFusion coupling, and focused tests cover labels/serialization.
acceptance_criteria: EffectCapability supports a generic external effect; metadata can represent one external-effect observation as stable JSON; docs describe usage and non-goals; focused cargo tests pass.
close_reason: Added EffectCapability::ExternalEffect with stable label support, ExternalEffectMetadataRecord/Kind with JSONL projection and regression/property coverage, runtime tracing label coverage, introspection effect-label reuse, and docs for metadata JSON plus contract/capability authoring and AI-call workload follow-up. Verified cargo fmt --all --check, cargo test -p conduit-core, cargo test -p conduit-runtime, cargo test -p conduit-introspection, focused clippy for touched crates, git diff --check, and cargo check --workspace --all-targets (passes with existing conduit-test-kit unused_mut warnings).

title: Create LLM version of architecture guide
description: Create a compact LLM-oriented version of the architecture guide under docs/llm with stable facts, terms, and boundaries for retrieval use.
close_reason: Created the LLM retrieval version of the architecture guide under docs/llm with compact topic pages for architecture, runtime, contracts, and doc tooling.

title: Epic 8: Real Workflow Execution
description: Replace scaffold execution with real workflow execution surfaces: executor registries, run summaries, observer-aware APIs, contract-aware output validation, and CLI/native examples that run declared node implementations instead of no-op placeholders.

title: executor-registry-trait
description: Add a NodeExecutorRegistry-style boundary so workflow execution can resolve declared nodes to concrete executors without hard-coding a single no-op executor.

title: workflow-run-summary
description: Introduce WorkflowRunSummary with terminal state, first error, cancelled node count, completed node count, and basic message/error counts for callers and CLI output.

title: run-workflow-with-observers-api
description: Consolidate workflow execution APIs around observer-aware execution that accepts lifecycle hooks and metadata sinks, returns WorkflowRunSummary, and leaves thin compatibility wrappers where useful.

title: contract-aware-output-validation
description: Validate node outputs against workflow contracts before packets enter downstream graph edges, keeping validation at the host engine boundary rather than inside BatchExecutor adapters.

title: cli-run-real-native-registry
description: Replace scaffold-noop CLI run with a small real native executor registry path so declared native nodes can be executed in examples and tests.

title: native-linear-etl-example
description: Add a runnable native source-transform-sink workflow example using the real execution path and documenting its metadata output.

title: Restore Mermaid diagrams in architecture guide
description: Reintroduce architecture-guide diagrams via checked Mermaid-rendered SVG assets so HTML and PDF outputs show the intended system overview, runtime, and boundary diagrams without the prior rendering artifact.
close_reason: Completed: added checked Mermaid source and SVG assets for the architecture guide, then re-rendered and verified HTML/PDF output

title: Polish architecture guide prose tone
description: Make the architecture guide read more like a narrative chapter by smoothing transitions and reducing checklist-like prose in the remaining sections.
close_reason: Polished the chapter prose with stronger transitions and more narrative framing, then re-rendered the architecture guide.

title: Epic 17: Workload Discovery Before Data Tier

Verified cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check -p conduit-engine --example fanout_fanin, cargo run -p conduit-engine --example fanout_fanin, strict clippy for the engine example, git diff --check, and CLI validate/explain for the workflow file.

Updated runtime lifecycle emission so cancellation errors record node_cancelled instead of node_failed, with regression coverage. Verified cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check/run/clippy for watcher_cancellation example, cargo test -p conduit-runtime, cargo test -p conduit-engine, git diff --check, and CLI validate/explain for the workflow file.

title: NodeExecutor contract alignment
description: Reconcile the scaffolded NodeExecutor trait with the proposal before the runtime grows further. Verso target: why the chosen contract is the right staging point before asupersync integration.

close_reason: Added workflow-capability cross-validation so node capability claims are checked against declared workflow nodes and port directions, with mismatch tests covering node, port, and direction failures.

title: bench-metadata-overhead
description: Benchmark NoopMetadataSink versus default/tiered sinks to guard the zero-cost-abstraction goal.

title: Epic 2: Runtime Bootstrap
description: Move Conduit from a type-complete scaffold into an honest runtime skeleton with observable execution, an explicit concurrency substrate, and the first real runtime supervision semantics.
close_reason: All Runtime Bootstrap child beads are closed.

title: Runtime lifecycle observation seam
description: Make conduit-runtime responsible for emitting lifecycle events through LifecycleHook so the observability seam becomes real before deeper runtime work lands. Acceptance: run_node dispatches NodeStarted before execution and NodeCompleted or NodeFailed after execution; a no-op default hook keeps existing callers simple; tests verify event ordering and that executor failures still propagate unchanged.
close_reason: Wired lifecycle observation through conduit-runtime with a no-op default hook, explicit hook-aware execution entry point, and runtime tests for started/completed/failed ordering and error propagation.

title: Asupersync runtime skeleton
description: Introduce asupersync as the runtime substrate in conduit-runtime and prove the first structured-concurrency slice against the current scaffold. Acceptance: asupersync is a declared dependency with the proposal/docs linked to the upstream repository; conduit-runtime owns a basic task-tree-backed execution entry point; cancellation and failure handling are expressed in code and tests at the level supported by the current node interface; the runtime remains intentionally narrow and does not yet claim full FBP semantics.
close_reason: Added asupersync as the runtime substrate, documented the upstream source in the proposal, and introduced a narrow asupersync-backed node execution wrapper with cancellation and failure tests.

title: Document Asupersync integration boundary
description: Capture the integration rule that Asupersync stays below Conduit-owned FBP APIs. Conduit should expose NodeContext, PortsIn, PortsOut, metadata, and capability surfaces rather than leaking Asupersync Cx or raw runtime primitives into public node contracts. Acceptance: docs or Verso note names the boundary, explains why Cx remains internal, and identifies where adapters are allowed.
close_reason: Documented Asupersync integration boundary: Conduit owns FBP public APIs and Asupersync remains an internal runtime adapter substrate.

title: Expose cancel-safe reserve commit output sends

close_reason: Added Conduit PortSendPermit reserve/commit flow over Asupersync send permits; dropping or aborting reserved output capacity releases slots without messages.

description: Move NodeContext cancellation beyond passive state by backing it with a Conduit-owned cancellation view that can be driven by Asupersync task context or handles. Acceptance: pre-cancelled contexts still reject execution; runtime-triggered cancellation is visible inside a running node; child node tasks are cancelled deterministically; public APIs do not expose Asupersync Cx directly.
close_reason: Added Conduit-owned cancellation handles/tokens, wired runtime execution through shared cancellation, and tested active cancellation visibility plus shared child-context rejection.

close_reason: Completed the final editorial wording pass for the architecture guide.

title: Epic 3: True FBP Core
description: Move Conduit from the sequential workflow scaffold to a true long-lived FBP process graph: async ports, concurrent supervision, edge capacity, cycle policy, and robust runtime tests. Source: docs/archetecture/proposal_final.md.

title: ports-async-recv-send
description: Add async recv, reserve, and send methods on PortsIn/PortsOut with cancellation mapping, while preserving try_* methods for deterministic tests.
close_reason: Implemented async PortsIn::recv plus PortsOut::reserve/send with cancellation-aware port errors and tests. Verified cargo fmt --all --check, cargo test -p conduit-core, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: cli-use-conduit-runtime
description: Replace the temporary CLI futures::executor execution path with AsupersyncRuntime so the CLI follows the same runtime boundary as production execution.
close_reason: CLI now uses AsupersyncRuntime for the scaffold entrypoint

title: ports-recv-any
description: Add recv_any for multi-input nodes, stream joins, watcher control inputs, and non-busy polling across declared input ports.
close_reason: Implemented PortsIn::recv_any for multi-input async receive, including cancellation, closed-input handling, and tests. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: engine-concurrent-supervisor
description: Replace the primary sequential workflow runner with supervised concurrent node execution: wire all edges first, spawn one task per node, and aggregate terminal results.
close_reason: Implemented concurrent workflow supervision by wiring all ports first, polling every node future concurrently, and aggregating terminal results in workflow order while preserving the borrowed NodeExecutor API. Added tests for bounded capacity-1 source/sink flow and all-node polling before terminal failure reporting. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: engine-fail-fast-cancellation
description: Attach one workflow cancellation handle to all node contexts and cancel sibling tasks on first node failure for the MVP failure policy.
close_reason: Attached one workflow cancellation handle to all node contexts and changed the supervisor to request cancellation on the first node failure while draining remaining terminal results. Added a sibling-cancellation regression test. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: workflow-edge-capacity
description: Add EdgeCapacity to EdgeDefinition, resolve Default to the engine default capacity, and wire explicit capacities into bounded edge channel construction.
close_reason: Added EdgeCapacity to workflow edges with default and explicit bounded variants, resolved default capacities at the engine boundary, and exposed an explicit-capacity builder for tests. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check --workspace --all-targets, cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -W clippy::pedantic -W clippy::nursery, and nix develop . --command cargo-dylint-nightly --all.

title: workflow-topology-diagnostics
description: Add topological ordering and CycleDetected diagnostics using a small hand-rolled graph algorithm before introducing any graph crate dependency.
close_reason: Workflow topology diagnostics implemented and validated

title: workflow-cycle-policy
description: Reject cycles by default and add an explicit cycles-allowed construction path for future feedback-loop workflows.

title: types-identifier-length-cap
description: Add a maximum identifier length and typed TooLong error to conduit-types to close the robustness gap called out in the final proposal.
close_reason: Identifier length cap added to conduit-types

title: engine-backpressure-tests
description: Add deterministic runtime tests proving bounded propagation, blocked sends, downstream receive unblocking, fan-out, fan-in, and closure propagation.

title: documentation-devshell-frankenmermaid-capability
description: Rename the Quarto-only devshell feature into a broader documentation feature and add Mermaid diagram rendering capability for future human/LLM documentation work. Acceptance: flake enables documentation feature instead of quarto, devshell documentation module includes Quarto plus frankenmermaid/fm-cli tooling or a pinned derivation, bead state is synced, and Nix evaluation confirms the documentation feature remains valid.

close_reason: Added documentation devshell feature with Quarto and pinned frankenmermaid/fm-cli tooling; verified devshell build and CLI smoke checks.
